findings.blocker: The load-bearing restrict-iso `(dual M).restrict f ≅ dual (M.restrict f)` is NOT a verbatim mirror of the closed `tensorObj_restrict_iso` past Step 3/H1. Empirically (lean_goal): Steps 1-3 + H1 typecheck cleanly; the residual is `(pushforward β).obj (dual A) ≅ dual ((pushforward β).obj A)` — open-immersion pushforward commuting with the presheaf dual. This does NOT close via H2'/restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv: the tensor's H2 worked because tensorObj is SECTIONWISE (a restrictScalars-image, so the strong-monoidal tensorator applies), but dual = internalHom(-,𝟙_) is the SLICE internal hom (value is a morphism-module over Over U, not a sectionwise restrictScalars-image), so it has no sectionwise strong-monoidal analogue. Closing it requires a Mathlib-absent open-immersion slice-site equivalence (~150-300 LOC). GENUINE block, not budget exhaustion.

attempts.strategy: Draft the requested verbatim mirror `dual_restrict_iso_SCRATCH` of the closed `tensorObj_restrict_iso`; reduce restriction to pullback (restrictFunctorIsoPullback), move pullback inside sheafification (sheafificationCompPullback), strip sheafification (.mapIso), apply H1 (pushforwardPushforwardAdj.leftAdjointUniq), then read off the residual via lean_goal.

attempts.lean_error: no error — Steps 1-3 + H1 typecheck; residual goal exposed: `⊢ (PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).obj M.val.dual ≅ (M.restrict f).val.dual`, i.e. `(pushforward β).obj (dual A) ≅ dual ((pushforward β).obj A)`
attempts.insight: The blueprint 'verbatim mirror' claim is correct ONLY through Step 3/H1. The residual is the slice-internal-hom-vs-sectionwise mismatch — H2' does not lift. Scratch removed (no sorry left).

next_steps: Route hard-blocked at H2'. Do NOT re-dispatch the verbatim-mirror route — it is empirically falsified.

attempts.insight: The isoMk naturality discharged by default `cat_disch` — the slice-restriction coherence is essentially definitional (Over.map g ⋙ Over.forget U = Over.forget V on .left). This confirms the dual IS cleanly contravariantly functorial in isos; the slice obstruction is specific to the open-immersion PUSHFORWARD commutation, not to iso-functoriality.
